architecture_pattern: Layered Architecture (Presentation, Business Logic, Data Access)
summary: A secure, scalable, and maintainable REST API for user registration and JWT-based authentication built with FastAPI. It enforces strong password hashing, token expiration, role-agnostic protected endpoints, and follows security-first principles while remaining horizontally scalable.

components.name: Authentication Service
components.responsibility: Handles user registration, login, JWT generation/verification, and password hashing/validation

components.name: User Repository
components.responsibility: CRUD operations for User entities; abstracts database interactions

components.name: Database Adapter
components.responsibility: Manages connection pooling, migrations, and transaction boundaries
components.technology: SQLAlchemy + asyncpg (for PostgreSQL) or aiosqlite (for SQLite)

components.name: Security Middleware
components.responsibility: Validates JWT tokens, enforces HTTPS in production, handles token refresh logic (future extension)

components.name: API Router
components.responsibility: Defines and mounts all public and protected endpoints with validation, docs (Swagger/ReDoc), and error handling

api_endpoints.description: Registers a new user with email and password
Authenticates user credentials and returns a JWT access token

tech_stack.backend: Python 3.11, FastAPI, SQLModel, Pydantic v2, python-jose[cryptography], bcrypt
tech_stack.cache: None (intentionally omitted per scope; optional Redis for future token revocation/blacklisting)

deployment_strategy: Containerized deployment via Docker on cloud platforms (AWS ECS/EKS or Heroku); uses environment variables for secrets (JWT_SECRET_KEY, DATABASE_URL); supports zero-downtime rolling updates; health checks via /health endpoint; CI/CD pipeline with automated tests and linting.
security_considerations: Passwords hashed using bcrypt with auto-generated salt (bcrypt.generate_password_hash)
JWT signed with HS256 and secret key stored in environment variable (never hardcoded)
JWT expiration enforced (default 1h), short-lived tokens recommended
Email validation via RFC-compliant regex (Pydantic EmailStr)
Rate limiting on auth endpoints (via slowapi or custom middleware) to prevent brute-force
SQL injection prevented via parameterized queries (SQLModel/SQLAlchemy)
Sensitive headers (e.g., Authorization) excluded from logs
scalability_notes: Stateless authentication enables horizontal scaling of API instances
Database connection pooling configured per FastAPI worker (asyncpg/aiosqlite)
Read replicas can be added for future read-heavy extensions
Async I/O throughout stack (FastAPI + async DB drivers) maximizes throughput
Docker image optimized for minimal attack surface (slim Python base, multi-stage build)
Health and metrics endpoints support autoscaling triggers (e.g., CPU/memory + request rate)
